title: Cloud Infrastructure Services Agreement
full_text:
CLOUD INFRASTRUCTURE SERVICES AGREEMENT

This Cloud Infrastructure Services Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of January 15, 2024, by and between Acme Corp, a Delaware corporation with principal offices at 100 Innovation Drive, San Francisco, CA 94105 ("Client"), and CloudScale Solutions Inc., a California corporation with principal offices at 200 Tech Boulevard, Mountain View, CA 94043 ("Provider").

1. DEFINITIONS

1.1 "Services" means the cloud computing infrastructure services described in Exhibit A, including virtual machine provisioning, storage allocation, network management, and monitoring capabilities.
1.2 "Service Level Agreement" or "SLA" means the performance standards set forth in Exhibit B, including uptime guarantees of 99.9% monthly availability.
1.3 "Confidential Information" means any non-public information disclosed by either party to the other in connection with this Agreement, whether in written, oral, electronic, or other form.
1.4 "Effective Date" means January 15, 2024.
1.5 "Term" means the initial period of twelve (12) months from the Effective Date, subject to renewal as described in Section 7.

2. SCOPE OF WORK

2.1 Provider shall deliver the Services as described in Exhibit A in accordance with the SLA.
2.2 Provider shall assign a dedicated account manager to Client within five (5) business days of the Effective Date.
2.3 Provider shall provide twenty-four (24) hour technical support via phone, email, and chat during the Term.
2.4 Client shall provide Provider with reasonable access to systems and information necessary for Provider to perform the Services.
2.5 Any changes to the scope of Services shall be documented in a written change order signed by both parties.

3. PAYMENT TERMS

3.1 Client shall pay Provider a monthly fee of $45,000 USD, due within thirty (30) days of receipt of invoice.
3.2 Provider shall submit invoices on the first business day of each calendar month for the preceding month's Services.
3.3 Late payments shall accrue interest at a rate of 1.5% per month or the maximum rate permitted by law, whichever is less.
3.4 Either party may dispute an invoice in good faith by providing written notice within fifteen (15) days of receipt. Undisputed amounts remain due as scheduled.
3.5 All fees are exclusive of applicable taxes, which shall be the responsibility of Client.

4. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

4.1 Each party retains all rights, title, and interest in its pre-existing intellectual property.
4.2 Any tools, methodologies, or processes developed by Provider in the course of delivering the Services shall remain the property of Provider.
4.3 Client retains all rights to its data stored on or processed through the Services.
4.4 Provider grants Client a non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use Provider's proprietary tools solely in connection with the Services during the Term.

5. CONFIDENTIALITY

5.1 Each party agrees to maintain the confidentiality of the other party's Confidential Information and to use it solely for the purposes of this Agreement.
5.2 Confidential Information shall not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the receiving party; (b) was known to the receiving party prior to disclosure; (c) is independently developed by the receiving party; or (d) is received from a third party without restriction.
5.3 Confidentiality obligations shall survive for a period of three (3) years following termination of this Agreement.

6. TERMINATION

6.1 Either party may terminate this Agreement for cause upon sixty (60) days written notice if the other party materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within the notice period.
6.2 Either party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon ninety (90) days written notice.
6.3 Upon termination, Provider shall assist Client with migration of data and services for a period of thirty (30) days at no additional cost.

7. LIABILITY

7.1 Neither party shall be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising out of or related to this Agreement.
7.2 Each party's total aggregate liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid or payable during the twelve (12) month period preceding the claim.
7.3 The limitations in this Section 7 shall not apply to breaches of confidentiality obligations or willful misconduct.

8. GOVERNING LAW

8.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflict of laws provisions.
8.2 Any disputes arising under this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in San Francisco, California, in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.
8.3 The prevailing party in any arbitration shall be entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys' fees and costs.

title: Enterprise Software Licensing and Customization Agreement
full_text:
ENTERPRISE SOFTWARE LICENSING AND CUSTOMIZATION AGREEMENT

This Enterprise Software Licensing and Customization Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of February 1, 2024, by and among GlobalTech Industries, a multinational corporation organized under the laws of Germany with US offices at 500 Commerce Street, Chicago, IL 60601 ("Licensee"), NexaFlow Software Ltd., an Irish company with offices at 15 Silicon Docks, Dublin 2, Ireland ("Licensor"), and DataBridge Consulting Group, a New York corporation with offices at 300 Park Avenue, New York, NY 10022 ("Implementation Partner").

1. DEFINITIONS

1.1 "Software" means NexaFlow Enterprise Suite version 8.x, including all modules listed in Schedule 1 and any updates or patches provided during the Term.
1.2 "Customizations" means any modifications, extensions, or integrations developed specifically for Licensee by Implementation Partner, as described in the Statement of Work attached as Schedule 2.
1.3 "Go-Live Date" means the date on which the Software and Customizations are deployed to Licensee's production environment, targeted for August 1, 2024.
1.4 "Acceptance Period" means thirty (30) calendar days following the Go-Live Date during which Licensee shall conduct acceptance testing.
1.5 "Licensed Users" means up to 2,500 named users as specified in Schedule 3, expandable in increments of 500 users.

2. SCOPE OF WORK

2.1 Licensor grants Licensee a non-exclusive, worldwide license to use the Software for internal business operations.
2.2 Implementation Partner shall deliver the Customizations according to the milestones in Schedule 2, with an overall project timeline of six (6) months.
2.3 Implementation Partner shall provide a project manager, two senior developers, and one QA engineer dedicated to the project for the duration of the implementation phase.
2.4 Licensee shall make available a dedicated project team including a project sponsor, business analyst, and technical lead.
2.5 Scope changes exceeding 10% of the original Statement of Work shall require a formal change request process and may result in additional fees and timeline adjustments.
2.6 The parties acknowledge that the Go-Live Date is a target and not a guaranteed delivery date. Delays caused by Licensee shall extend the timeline proportionally.

3. PAYMENT TERMS

3.1 Licensee shall pay Licensor an annual license fee of $850,000 USD, payable in advance within fifteen (15) days of each anniversary of the Effective Date.
3.2 Licensee shall pay Implementation Partner according to the following milestone schedule: 25% upon contract signing ($312,500), 25% upon completion of design phase, 25% upon Go-Live, and 25% upon acceptance.
3.3 Implementation Partner's hourly rate for out-of-scope work is $275 USD per hour.
3.4 If Licensee fails to make any payment within fifteen (15) days of the due date, Licensor may suspend access to the Software upon five (5) business days written notice.
3.5 Annual license fees shall increase by the greater of 5% or the Consumer Price Index (CPI) adjustment on each renewal anniversary.
3.6 Implementation Partner may invoice for reasonable travel expenses incurred in connection with on-site work, not to exceed $15,000 per quarter without prior approval.

4. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

4.1 Licensor retains all rights, title, and interest in the Software, including any derivative works.
4.2 All Customizations developed by Implementation Partner shall be jointly owned by Licensee and Implementation Partner. Implementation Partner may reuse generalized components in other client engagements provided they do not include Licensee's proprietary business logic.
4.3 Licensee grants Licensor and Implementation Partner a limited, non-exclusive license to use Licensee's trademarks and trade names solely for the purpose of performing obligations under this Agreement.
4.4 Licensee assigns to Licensor any feedback, suggestions, or enhancement requests provided regarding the Software's core functionality.

5. CONFIDENTIALITY

5.1 Each party agrees to protect the Confidential Information of the other parties using the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care.
5.2 Confidentiality obligations shall survive for five (5) years following termination, except for trade secrets which shall be protected indefinitely.
5.3 Notwithstanding the above, each party may disclose Confidential Information to its affiliates, subcontractors, and professional advisors who have a need to know, provided they are bound by confidentiality obligations no less restrictive than those in this Agreement.

6. TERMINATION

6.1 The initial license term is three (3) years from the Effective Date, automatically renewing for successive one (1) year periods unless either party provides one hundred twenty (120) days written notice of non-renewal.
6.2 Licensor may terminate this Agreement immediately if Licensee fails to pay any undisputed amount within thirty (30) days of the due date.
6.3 Upon termination, Licensee shall cease all use of the Software within thirty (30) days and certify destruction of all copies.
6.4 Customizations owned by Licensee may continue to be used by Licensee after termination, subject to any third-party license restrictions.

7. LIABILITY

7.1 Licensor's total liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the annual license fees paid in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim.
7.2 Implementation Partner's total liability shall not exceed the total fees paid to Implementation Partner under this Agreement.
7.3 NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR ANY LOSS OF PROFITS, LOSS OF DATA, BUSINESS INTERRUPTION, OR ANY INDIRECT, SPECIAL, INCIDENTAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, HOWEVER CAUSED.
7.4 Licensee acknowledges that the Software is provided "as-is" with respect to compatibility with Licensee's existing systems not expressly listed in Schedule 1.
7.5 Implementation Partner warrants that Customizations will conform to the specifications in Schedule 2 for a period of ninety (90) days following acceptance.

8. GOVERNING LAW

8.1 This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York, without regard to its conflict of laws provisions.
8.2 Any disputes shall first be subject to good faith negotiation for thirty (30) days, followed by mediation administered by JAMS in New York City. If mediation fails, disputes shall be resolved by litigation in the state or federal courts located in Manhattan, New York.

title: Managed Security Operations and Threat Response Agreement
full_text:
MANAGED SECURITY OPERATIONS AND THREAT RESPONSE AGREEMENT

This Managed Security Operations and Threat Response Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of March 1, 2024, by and between Pinnacle Financial Services, a federally chartered bank with headquarters at 1200 Wall Street, New York, NY 10005 ("Client"), and IronVault Cybersecurity Inc., a Delaware corporation with offices at 45 Fortress Lane, Arlington, VA 22201 ("Provider").

1. DEFINITIONS

1.1 "Security Services" means the managed detection and response (MDR), security operations center (SOC) monitoring, vulnerability management, and incident response services described in Exhibit A.
1.2 "Critical Incident" means any security event classified as Severity 1 or Severity 2 under the incident classification matrix in Exhibit B, including confirmed data breaches, active ransomware attacks, and unauthorized access to systems containing customer financial data.
1.3 "Response Time" means the maximum elapsed time from Provider's detection of a Critical Incident to initial containment actions, which shall not exceed fifteen (15) minutes for Severity 1 events and sixty (60) minutes for Severity 2 events.
1.4 "Regulatory Requirements" means all applicable federal and state laws, regulations, and guidance including but not limited to GLBA, SOX, PCI-DSS, NYDFS Cybersecurity Regulation (23 NYCRR 500), and OCC guidelines.
1.5 "Data" means all Client information processed, stored, or transmitted through Provider's systems in connection with the Security Services.

2. SCOPE OF WORK

2.1 Provider shall deliver 24/7/365 security monitoring and threat detection across all Client networks, endpoints, cloud environments, and critical applications listed in Exhibit C.
2.2 Provider shall maintain a dedicated team of no fewer than three (3) security analysts assigned exclusively to Client's environment at all times.
2.3 Provider shall achieve full operational capability within forty-five (45) calendar days of the Effective Date. Failure to meet this deadline shall result in a service credit of $25,000 per week of delay, deducted from the following month's invoice.
2.4 Provider shall conduct quarterly penetration testing and provide remediation reports within five (5) business days of test completion.
2.5 Provider shall integrate with Client's existing SIEM platform (Splunk Enterprise) and ticketing system (ServiceNow) within the first thirty (30) days.
2.6 Client shall grant Provider administrative access to all security tools and network segments necessary for Provider to perform the Services.

3. PAYMENT TERMS

3.1 Client shall pay Provider a monthly fee of $185,000 USD, due within ten (10) business days of receipt of invoice.
3.2 Incident response engagements beyond the included twenty (20) hours per month shall be billed at $450 USD per hour.
3.3 Late payments shall incur a penalty of 2.5% of the outstanding balance per month, compounding monthly.
3.4 Provider reserves the right to suspend non-critical monitoring services if payment is more than fifteen (15) days overdue, upon forty-eight (48) hours written notice.
3.5 Annual fee increases shall not exceed 8% per year, applied on each anniversary of the Effective Date.
3.6 Client shall pay a one-time onboarding fee of $150,000 USD due upon contract execution, which is non-refundable under any circumstances.

4. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

4.1 Provider retains all rights to its proprietary detection rules, threat intelligence feeds, automation playbooks, and analytical methodologies.
4.2 Any custom detection rules, response playbooks, or security configurations developed specifically for Client shall be owned exclusively by Provider. Client receives a non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use such materials solely during the Term.
4.3 Client hereby assigns to Provider all rights to any threat intelligence, attack patterns, or security insights derived from monitoring Client's environment. Provider may use such intelligence to improve its services for all clients.
4.4 Upon termination, Client shall have no right to retain, copy, or use any Provider materials, including custom configurations developed for Client's environment.

5. CONFIDENTIALITY

5.1 Provider acknowledges that it will have access to highly sensitive financial data, customer personally identifiable information (PII), and proprietary trading algorithms. Provider agrees to implement security controls meeting or exceeding the requirements of NIST SP 800-171 for all Client data.
5.2 Provider shall not disclose any information about Client's security posture, vulnerabilities, or incidents to any third party without Client's prior written consent, except as required by law or regulation.
5.3 Confidentiality obligations under this section shall survive in perpetuity.
5.4 Provider shall immediately notify Client of any breach or suspected breach of confidentiality within two (2) hours of discovery.

6. TERMINATION

6.1 The initial term is thirty-six (36) months from the Effective Date. This Agreement shall automatically renew for successive twenty-four (24) month periods unless Client provides written notice of non-renewal at least one hundred eighty (180) days prior to the end of the then-current term.
6.2 Provider may terminate this Agreement immediately if Client fails to pay any invoice within thirty (30) days of the due date or if Client fails to provide required system access within ten (10) business days of Provider's written request.
6.3 If Client terminates for convenience during the initial term, Client shall pay an early termination fee equal to 75% of the remaining monthly fees for the balance of the initial term.
6.4 Upon termination for any reason, Provider shall have no obligation to assist with transition or migration of services. Any transition assistance requested shall be billed at $500 USD per hour.
6.5 Provider may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days notice if it determines, in its sole discretion, that continued performance poses an unacceptable risk to Provider's business or reputation.

7. LIABILITY

7.1 Provider's total aggregate liability for all claims arising under this Agreement shall not exceed the fees paid by Client in the three (3) months immediately preceding the event giving rise to the claim.
7.2 IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER BE LIABLE FOR ANY DATA BREACH, DATA LOSS, UNAUTHORIZED ACCESS, OR SECURITY INCIDENT, REGARDLESS OF WHETHER PROVIDER WAS NEGLIGENT OR FAILED TO MEET THE SERVICE LEVELS IN THIS AGREEMENT.
7.3 Client shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Provider from any and all claims, damages, losses, and expenses (including attorneys' fees) arising from or related to: (a) Client's use of the Services; (b) any regulatory action or investigation involving Client; (c) any third-party claims related to Client's data or systems.
7.4 Client acknowledges that security services cannot guarantee the prevention of all security incidents and that Provider's failure to detect or prevent a specific threat shall not constitute a breach of this Agreement.
7.5 The limitations in this Section 7 shall apply regardless of the form of action, whether in contract, tort, strict liability, or otherwise, and shall survive termination.

8. GOVERNING LAW

8.1 This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to its conflict of laws provisions.
8.2 All disputes shall be resolved exclusively through binding arbitration conducted by a single arbitrator in Wilmington, Delaware, under the rules of the American Arbitration Association. The arbitrator shall have no authority to award punitive or exemplary damages.
8.3 Each party waives any right to a jury trial in connection with any dispute arising under this Agreement.
8.4 The prevailing party shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys' fees, expert witness fees, and all costs of arbitration from the non-prevailing party.
